description: Add chaos/stress tests to search, accordion, theme, and port-related side panel E2E tests. Covers rapid input cycling, boundary values, and interleaved UI actions. Also creates the cross-domain stress test file.

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-search-details.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for rapid type/clear cycles, progressive typing, long input, and clear button mashing.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-search-details.e2e.ts
Rapid type/clear test fills and clears search input 10x with 50ms between each, then verifies all plugins are visible (no zombie filter state)
Long input test fills 200 chars, verifies no freeze, clears and verifies recovery
All stress tests use page.on('pageerror') and assert zero errors
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-search-details.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-search-details.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests no-results empty state, search filtering, and clear button. The search input is located via `sp.locator('input[type="text"]').first()`. The clear button is the X icon next to the search input.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with inline helpers:
```
const tick = (ms = 100) => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms));
const collectPageErrors = (page: Page): string[] => { const errors: string[] = []; page.on('pageerror', (err: Error) => errors.push(err.message)); return errors; };
```

Test 1 — rapid type/clear: Fill 'slack', wait 50ms, fill '', wait 50ms. Repeat 10x. After final clear, expect(sp.getByText('E2E Test')).toBeVisible(). Then type progressively: s → sl → sla → slac → slack with 30ms between each. Wait 500ms for debounce. Clear and verify clean.

Test 2 — long input: Fill 'a'.repeat(200), wait 300ms, clear. Fill 'zzz_nonexistent_plugin_xyz', wait 500ms, clear, verify E2E Test visible.

Constraints:
- Follow manual setup pattern from existing tests
- Do NOT modify existing tests
- Import `type { Page }` from '@playwright/test' if not already imported

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-accordion-persist.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for rapid accordion expand/collapse (20x per card, interleaved) and verify persistence survives rapid toggling.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-accordion-persist.e2e.ts
Rapid toggle test clicks e2e card 20x, browser card 20x, and interleaves 10x, then verifies both cards are still functional (expandable, content visible)
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-accordion-persist.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-accordion-persist.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests accordion state persistence via chrome.storage.session. Plugin cards are located via `sp.locator('button[aria-expanded]').filter({ hasText: 'E2E Test' })` and `sp.locator('button[aria-expanded]').filter({ hasText: 'Browser' })`.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with rapid toggle test. Setup: standard MCP server + extension + side panel. Then loop 20x clicking e2eCard with 30ms tick between. Loop 20x clicking browserCard. Loop 10x alternating: e2eCard.click(), browserCard.click(), tick(30). After storm, check aria-expanded and click to expand if needed. Verify Echo tool visible in expanded e2e card. Verify browser_list_tabs permission select visible in expanded browser card.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify existing tests
- The card locator uses `button[aria-expanded]` filter — make sure both cards are found

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-theme.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for rapid theme toggle (20x) and theme persistence after fast close/reopen.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-theme.e2e.ts
Rapid toggle test clicks theme button 20x with 30ms between, then verifies plugin card is still visible
Fast close test toggles theme, closes side panel within 100ms, reopens, and verifies theme persisted
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-theme.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-theme.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests theme toggle and persistence. The theme toggle is located via `sp.locator('[aria-label="Switch to dark mode"], [aria-label="Switch to light mode"]')`. Theme is persisted in chrome.storage.local.

Changes:
Test 1 — rapid toggle: Click theme button 20x with 30ms tick. After storm, verify sp.getByText('E2E Test').toBeVisible(). Read html element class to verify dark/light is deterministic (even number of clicks = same as start).

Test 2 — fast close persistence: Record initial theme (check html class for 'dark'). Toggle theme once. Close side panel (sp.close()) within 100ms. Reopen via openSidePanel(context). Check html class matches new theme.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify existing tests
- Theme toggle aria-label changes between 'Switch to dark mode' and 'Switch to light mode' — use both in locator

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for port boundary values (0, 99999, same port re-entry) and rapid port changes.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts
Boundary test verifies port 0 clamps to >= 1, port 99999 clamps to <= 65535 on blur
Same port test enters current port value, verifies no-op (no unnecessary reconnect)
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests NumberStepper port editor reconnection to different servers. The port input is located via `sp.locator('input[type="number"]')`. It has min=1, max=65535. Values are committed on blur or Enter.

Changes:
Test 1 — boundary values: Read originalPort = await portInput.inputValue(). Fill '0', blur, verify inputValue >= 1. Fill '99999', blur, verify inputValue <= 65535. Fill originalPort, blur to restore.

Test 2 — same port: Read current port. Fill same value. Blur. Verify no disconnect (side panel still shows plugin cards, not 'Cannot Reach MCP Server').

Constraints:
- ALWAYS restore original port at end of test to not break connection for cleanup
- Do NOT modify existing tests
- The NumberStepper commits on blur — must call portInput.blur() after fill

userStories.title: Create side-panel-stress.e2e.ts cross-domain barrage test
userStories.description: Create a new test file that tests interleaved permission toggles + search + accordion + theme in a single test, verifying no crashes and server state consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: New file e2e/side-panel-stress.e2e.ts exists
Test interleaves: permission toggles (Off/Auto on plugin and browser), theme toggles, search fill/clear, accordion expand/collapse across 3 rounds
After barrage, verifies both permission selects show 'Auto', mcpClient.listTools() confirms tools enabled, plugin cards visible
Test asserts zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-stress.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-stress.e2e.ts (NEW file)

Context:
This is the cross-domain stress test that doesn't belong in any single existing file. It combines permission toggles, search, accordion, and theme in interleaved sequence.

Changes:
Create new file with standard imports from fixtures.js and helpers.js. Single test.describe('Side panel stress test') with one test. Setup: standard MCP server (with OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS='') + mcpClient + extension + side panel. Expand both e2e and browser cards.

Round 1 — permission + theme:
```
await selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for e2e-test plugin', 'Off');
await themeToggle.click();
await selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for browser tools', 'Off');
await themeToggle.click();
await selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for e2e-test plugin', 'Auto');
await selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for browser tools', 'Auto');
```

Round 2 — accordion + search + theme:
Collapse/expand cards, fill/clear search, toggle theme.

Round 3 — rapid permission + accordion:
selectPermission Ask, click cards, selectPermission Auto.

After barrage: clear search, set both permissions to Auto, verify UI shows Auto, poll mcpClient.listTools() to verify e2e-test_echo and browser_list_tabs not disabled. Assert pageErrors empty.

Constraints:
- IMPORTANT: Do not use search fill while permissions are being toggled — Radix Select can't open when search filters hide the plugin cards
- Use `sp.getByText('Browser', { exact: true }).first()` to avoid strict mode violation (tool descriptions contain 'browser')
- Mark plugin as reviewed in config: reviewedVersion: pluginVersion
